subject: Re: Re: 3D denoising - output black reconstruction
body:
Okay - small update

Log loss is stable up to iteration 800 or so, reconstruction works here. 
Somewhere around iteration 3000 the log(loss) goes to negative infinity 
which must be the source of the problems. I'm trying different batch sizes 
and learning rates now.

Best,

Alister

On Friday, 17 January 2020 10:21:58 UTC+1, Alister Burt wrote:
>
> Hi Dmitry,
>
>  
>
> Thanks for the swift reply – appreciated! I’ll download the new installer 
> and give things ago before updating you. 
>
>  
>
> I’ve not managed to get anything denoised yet unfortunately, I’ll keep 
> plugging away!
>
>  
>
> Thanks for the tips re: learning rate and faster testing.
>
>  
>
> Cheers,
>
>  
>
> Alister
>
>  
>
> Sent from Mail <https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkId=550986> for 
> Windows 10
>
>  
>
> *From: *Dimitry Tegunov <tegunov@gmail.com>
> *Sent: *Friday, January 17, 2020 12:40 AM
> *To: *Warp <warp-em@googlegroups.com>
> *Subject: *Re: 3D denoising - output black reconstruction
>
>  
>
> Ouch, 60k iterations! Sorry it didn't lead to a result :-( I can't 
> remember if I ever tested batchsize = 1, but the training gradient might 
> become quite unstable in that case. Have you previously succeeded in 
> denoising anything with that configuration? There is a new version of 
> Noise2Map in this installer: 
> https://www.dropbox.com/s/issib5n0zmtqbc6/Warp_20200114.msi?dl=0. It has 
> a couple of new parameters, but the user guide hasn't caught up yet. The 
> descriptions in the help text might be enough though. Briefly, there are 
> multiple networks of different width included. Maybe try --start_model 
> noisenet3dmodel_64, that's the narrowest one. Train for a few hundred 
> iterations and check if the loss value displayed during training 
> experiences sudden changes or even goes NaN, and if the output from 
> denoising is still black. If this happens early during training, try 
> setting --learningrate_start lower than its default value.
>
>  
>
> Cheers,
>
> Dimitry
>
> On Thursday, January 16, 2020 at 10:20:21 AM UTC+1, Alister Burt wrote:

> Hi all,
>
>  
>
> Does anyone have experience with using 3D denoising to denoise tomograms 
> with noise2map? I ran it on one tomogram with the following command
>
>  
>
> noise2map --observation1 even --observation2 odd --dont_flatten_spectrum 
> --iterations 15000 --batchsize 1 --gpuid_network 0 --gpuid_preprocess 0
>
>  
>

>

>

>
> Alister
